channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: White House says Trump ‘satisfied’ with blockade after Iran says it seized two ships in strait of
item.description: Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The White House said Trump is satisfied with the blockade. Middle East crisis live: White House says Trump ‘satisfied’ with blockade after Iran says it seized two ships in strait of Hormuz The Guardian.

item.title: Virtual Panel on United States-South Africa Relations: Ambassador Bozell and a Shifting Global
item.description: Ambassador Bozell discussed United States-South Africa relations at a virtual panel. Virtual Panel on United States-South Africa Relations: Ambassador Bozell and a Shifting Global Landscape Global Atlanta.

item.title: Man City go top and relegate Burnley with narrow Turf Moor win
item.description: Manchester City defeated Burnley with a narrow win at Turf Moor. Man City go top and relegate Burnley with narrow Turf Moor win.

item.title: Trump doesn’t view Iran’s seizing of non-US ships as a ceasefire violation, White House says
item.description: The White House said Trump does not view Iran's seizure of non-US ships as a ceasefire violation. Trump doesn’t view Iran’s seizing of non-US ships as a ceasefire violation, White House says CNN.

item.title: Iranian Forces Claim to Seize 2 Ships Near Strait of Hormuz After Trump Extends Truce
item.description: Iranian forces seized two ships near the Strait of Hormuz after Trump extended a truce. Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Forces Claim to Seize 2 Ships Near Strait of Hormuz After Trump Extends Truce The New York Times.

item.title: Pope Leo urges justice reform in Equatorial Guinea’s prisons
item.description: Pope Leo urged justice reform in Equatorial Guinea's prisons during his visit. Equatorial Guinea's prison conditions face scrutiny as Pope Leo pushes for humane treatment and reforms during his visit.

item.title: Iran attacks ships in Strait of Hormuz as thousands more U.S. forces head for Middle East
item.description: Iran attacked ships in the Strait of Hormuz as US forces headed to the Middle East. Live Updates: Iran attacks ships in Strait of Hormuz as thousands more U. S. forces head for Middle East CBS News.

item.title: Iran attacks 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz as Trump indefinitely extends ceasefire
item.description: Iran attacked three ships in the Strait of Hormuz as Trump extended a ceasefire indefinitely. Live updates: Iran attacks 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz as Trump indefinitely extends ceasefire AP News.

item.title: 2 Journalists Injured In Israeli Airstrike In Lebanon
item.description: Two Al-Akhbar journalists were injured in an Israeli airstrike in Lebanon. The Lebanese daily newspaper Al-Akhbar said its two journalists were injured in Israeli strike. 2 Journalists Injured In Israeli Airstrike In Lebanon.

item.title: 'Naughty and Nice' List By White House To Assess NATO Allies Over Iran War Stance
item.description: The White House is assessing NATO allies' stances on Iran using a list. 'Naughty and Nice' List By White House To Assess NATO Allies Over Iran War Stance Republic World.

item.title: Iran Says Reopening Strait of Hormuz 'Not Possible' Under US Naval Blockade
item.description: Iran has stated it cannot reopen the Strait of Hormuz under a US naval blockade. The statement comes after the US-Iran conflict began on February 28. US-Iran war broke out on February 28.

item.title: To Iran, Trump Blinked First by Extending the Cease-Fire
item.description: Iran's leaders believe they can outlast President Trump in a standoff, which could devastate average Iranians economically. Iran’s leaders believe that they can withstand an enduring standoff longer than President Trump.

item.title: Two Resignations, Border Anger: Trouble Mounts For Nepal's New Government
item.description: Nepal's new government faces instability with two resignations and border anger. Since Balen Shah took office, his government has been rocked by instability. Two Resignations, Border Anger: Trouble Mounts For Nepal's New Government.

item.title: Nepal Home Minister Sudan Gurung Resigns In Weeks Over Financial Transactions
item.description: Nepal Home Minister Sudan Gurung resigned over financial transactions. Gurung is the second minister to leave the new government. Nepal Home Minister Sudan Gurung Resigns In Weeks Over Financial Transactions.

item.title: US Stocks Climb Toward Records On Strong Earnings Despite Rising Oil Prices
item.description: US stocks climbed toward records on strong earnings despite rising oil prices. In the bond market, Treasury yields eased despite the gain in oil prices. US Stocks Climb Toward Records On Strong Earnings Despite Rising Oil Prices.

item.title: US Withholding Iraq’s Own Oil Money Over Ties to Iran
item.description: The US halted dollar shipments to Iraq's economy to force the government to distance itself from Iran. The United States has halted dollar shipments to Iraq’s cash-based economy in an effort to force the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Won't Execute 8 Women After His Request
item.description: Trump has secured a temporary reprieve for eight women facing execution in Iran. The fate of US-Iran talks hosted by Pakistan remains unclear. Trump Says Iran Won't Execute 8 Women After His Request.

item.title: South Korean arrested outside Yasukuni Shrine ceremony for war dead
item.description: A South Korean national was arrested outside Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine for holding a political banner. Police said a South Korean national holding a banner with political messages was arrested at Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine to Japan's war dead.

item.title: EU unblocks €90 billion Ukraine loan after Hungary row
item.description: EU finance ministers have agreed to unblock a €90 billion loan to Ukraine after resolving a dispute with Hungary. The move comes as Kyiv reports the restart of operations at the Druzhba pipeline, which Hungary relies on for Russian oil imports.

item.title: Pope tells prisoners in Equatorial Guinea 'you are not alone'
item.description: Pope Leo visited prisoners in Equatorial Guinea, telling them they are not alone, during his four-nation Africa tour. Pope Leo's four-nation Africa tour has pushed towards ​a dramatic close, with the pontiff braving a heavy rainstorm to greet prisoners in Equatorial Guinea.
